title: rosa gallica subsp. gallicosempervirens
intro: Rosa gallica subsp. gallicosempervirens, also known as the Gallica rose or French rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa gallica plant, native to southern Europe and known for its fragrant, pale pink flowers and rich, slightly sweet scent. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rosa gallica subsp. gallicosempervirens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from a range of health issues.
